rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE D J H DATED JULY 24, 1990 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To First Amended Probate Code Sec. 850
rulings.Rulings: Overruled as to the property claim causes of action 1-3 (850, quiet title, and declaratory relief) and cause of action 12 (for an accounting). Sustained without leave to amend as to all other causes of action for lack of standing since Petitioners have no interest in Pongracz' property except through the terms of the trust. If the Court determines the property that is the subject of the 850 claim belongs in the trust, they will receive their full share of it. If the Court determines it does not belong to the trust, the Court will necessarily have determined that no compensable injury was done to them by its removal from the trust. They are not otherwise succesors in interest or interested persons under the statutes to which they refer, and they have suggested no basis on which they could amend their petition to change that result. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, October 3, 2107.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES T. PONGRACZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Probate Code Sec. 850 Petition
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2 pm to be heard by Judge Busch; calendared at 9am in error. The demurrer is overruled as to the first three causes of action relating to the ownership of property (850, quiet title, declaratory relief). Petitioners have standing for the limited purpose of bringing the 850 petition pursuant to Probate Code section 850(a)(2)(C) as parties claiming an interest in property to which the decedent held title at the time of his death. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all remaining causes of action because Petitioners are not otherwise interested parties to this probate proceeding and have no basis to succeed to any property through probate. If the property that is the subject of the 850 petition is determined to belong to the trust, their rights will be entirely determined in the parallel trust proceeding. To the extent the property is ultimately determined to belong to this estate, Petitioners will have no rights to it or anything else in the estate. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, October 3, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES T. PONGRACZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Denied. The petition was not brought in bad faith or for purposes of delay, and there is at least a non-frivolous basis for some of the relief sought. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, October 3, 2107.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES T. PONGRACZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And For Discovery Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Denied with respect to the document inspection demands given that the documents have been produced (and were mailed prior to the filing of this motion). Granted as to special interrogatory 25. Objections were waived by untimely service and, in any event, the objections would be overruled. Petitioners Duncan and Biddinger have had a pleading pending in this matter continually from the time the discovery was propounded to the present. The information sought reasonably may lead to admissible evidence. In any event, objections raised for the first time in a response to a motion to compel are not appropriately considered. Petitioners Duncan and Biddinger must fully respond to this interrogatory within 15 days. Petitioners must also pay sanctions in the amount of $810 to Respondent Lewallen, reflecting the Court's determination of reasonable fees and costs to litigate the interrogatory issue as to which there was no substantial justification for failing to respond. No sanctions are allowed with respect to the document inspection issues as to which the documents were provided, and the motion was not needed to obtain that production. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the schuduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, October 3, 2017.
